name: auto-refresh: failure of one source does not abort refreshing the others (errors collected and reported at end)
command: mkdir -p .claude-tdd-pro/standards-last-fetch && for id in good-source bad-source; do echo "2026-05-11T00:00:00Z" > ".claude-tdd-pro/standards-last-fetch/$id.txt"; done && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/standards/auto-refresh-daily.sh" --now 2026-05-13T08:00:00Z --upstream-stub "$PWD/maybe-fail.sh" --report 2>out.txt && grep "good-source.*ok" out.txt 1>&2 && grep "bad-source.*failed" out.txt 1>&2 && echo ok 1>&2
setup: printf '#!/bin/bash\nif echo "$@" | grep -q bad-source; then exit 1; fi; echo ok; exit 0\n' > maybe-fail.sh && chmod +x maybe-fail.sh
